ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic milk tea concept (FOODON_03301103). No specific entry exists for oat milk teh melaka; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers tea-based beverages broadly. No specific code for oat milk teh melaka; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 ml (300 g) as typically served in a cup or glass at a Singaporean kopitiam or café chain such as Toast Box.

nutrient_source.title: Teh C (Tea with Evaporated Milk) and Oat Milk compositional data — estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central oat milk reference
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo) and USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Oat Milk Teh Melaka exists in HPB Foodo or USDA. Values estimated by combining: (1) HPB Foodo teh tarik / teh C reference for black tea with milk base, (2) USDA FoodData Central oat milk (unsweetened, ~45 kcal/100 ml), and (3) gula melaka (palm sugar) contribution. The beverage is approximately 60% oat milk, 30% brewed black tea, and 10% gula melaka syrup by volume. Per-100 g values are derived from this weighted blend. Confidence is medium due to recipe variability across outlets.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of oat milk and sweetened milk tea beverages — analogous estimate based on oat milk GI (GI ~49) and added palm sugar contribution
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Oat Milk Teh Melaka. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) oat milk GI ~49 (low-medium, University of Sydney GI Database, Wolever et al.); (2) gula melaka (palm sugar) GI ~35–54 (low-medium); (3) black tea has negligible GI contribution. Weighted blend of oat milk and palm sugar components yields an estimated composite GI of approximately 55. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (55 × 35.4 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 19.5 (medium). This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~114 mg per 300 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Oat Milk Teh Melaka contains approximately 114 mg sodium per standard 300 ml serving, which is within acceptable limits for hypertensive individuals when consumed occasionally, but added sugar from gula melaka should be monitored as excess sugar intake can indirectly elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added palm sugar (gula melaka) contributing significant free sugars (~25.5 g per 300 ml serving) and medium glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid Oat Milk Teh Melaka due to its substantial free sugar content from gula melaka (~25.5 g per serving) and a medium estimated glycemic load of 19.5, despite a low estimated glycemic index of 55; requesting reduced sugar or sugar-free preparation is advisable to manage postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content with oat milk base providing beta-glucan
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Oat Milk Teh Melaka has a favourable saturated fat profile (~0.6 g per 300 ml serving) and the oat milk base contributes beta-glucan, which is associated with modest LDL cholesterol reduction; however, the high added sugar content from gula melaka warrants moderation for cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Oat Milk Teh Melaka is a Singaporean and Malaysian-inspired hot or iced milk tea beverage that substitutes traditional dairy milk with oat milk and uses gula melaka (Malaccan palm sugar) as the sweetener in place of refined white sugar or condensed milk. The drink is built on a base of strongly brewed black tea — typically Ceylon or a local kopitiam blend — to which oat milk is added for a creamy, slightly nutty, and subtly sweet flavour profile. Gula melaka imparts a distinctive caramel-like, earthy sweetness with hints of coconut and molasses that differentiates this beverage from standard teh tarik or teh C. The texture is smooth and lightly creamy, lighter in body than a full-dairy milk tea. It is commonly served at Singaporean café chains such as Toast Box and is positioned as a plant-based or dairy-alternative option within the kopitiam beverage tradition. Nutritionally, the beverage provides moderate carbohydrates primarily from palm sugar (~35 g per 300 ml serving), low saturated fat due to the oat milk base, and a small amount of beta-glucan from oats. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 55 (low), with a medium glycemic load of 19.5 per serving. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution due to the free sugar content from gula melaka and the medium glycemic load, while those managing cardiovascular risk may benefit from the oat milk component's beta-glucan content. The beverage is halal-certified in most outlets and suitable for lacto-vegetarians.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Oat Milk Teh Melaka exists in HPB Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of oat milk (USDA FoodData Central), black tea, and gula melaka compositional data. GI is an analogous estimate based on oat milk and palm sugar GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to recipe variability across outlets and the absence of a direct laboratory analysis for this specific beverage.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component ingredients (oat milk ~60%, brewed black tea ~30%, gula melaka syrup ~10% by volume) using HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central references. Direct laboratory analysis of the Toast Box Oat Milk Teh Melaka product is recommended for high-confidence values. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reliably reported in the component source databases for this beverage type and no direct product analysis is available. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as oat milk and palm sugar contain no industrially produced trans fats; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central oat milk reference. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as oat milk is a plant-based product containing no cholesterol; naturally zero. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 as black tea and oat milk contain negligible vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 6. GI is set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement is not available. Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as (55 × 35.4) / 100 = 19.5 (medium). 7. The beverage may contain gluten from oats and is not suitable for coeliac individuals. 8. Halal status is based on standard Toast Box product positioning; individual outlet verification is recommended.
